title: myTax 2026 Foreign entities
description: How to complete your tax return using myTax when you have a direct or indirect interest in a foreign entity.
keywords: Foreign entity, myTax, online tax return, foreign entities, etax

content_blocks.text: Complete this section if you're an Australian resident for tax purposes who either:

items: had either a direct or an indirect interest in a controlled foreign company (CFC)
at any time, directly or indirectly transferred or caused the transfer of property (including money) or services to a non-resident trust.

content_blocks.text: If you're a temporary resident, don't show your foreign investment income at this section. See Tax-free income for temporary residents for the definition of a temporary resident and details of the exemption.
The CFC measures may apply to income and gains of foreign companies in which you alone, or with associates, had either:

items: direct or indirect controlling interest totalling 10% or more in a foreign company
effective control of a foreign company.

content_blocks.text: For more information, see Attribution of the current year profits of a controlled foreign company (CFC) .
Income from transfer of property (including money) or services to a non-resident trust estate

The transferor trust measures may apply if, at any time, you directly or indirectly transferred or caused the transfer of property (including money) or services to a non-resident trust. A trust is a non-resident trust if at any time during the income year both of the following applied:

items: no trustee of the trust estate was an Australian resident
the trust estate's central management and control wasn't in Australia.

content_blocks.text: For more information, see Transferor trust and related measures .
To personalise your tax return to show foreign entities, at Personalise return select:
To show your foreign entities details, at Prepare return select 'Add/Edit' at the Foreign income, assets and entities banner.

items: Enter your attributed foreign income from any CFC at Controlled foreign company (CFC) income .
Enter your attributed foreign income from transferor trusts at Income from transfer of property (including money) or services to a non-resident trust estate .

If you enter an amount at Income from transfer of property (including money) or services to a non-resident trust estate , you need to enter the Name of non-resident trust Name of trustee or trustees .
Select Save and continue when you have completed the Foreign income, assets and entities section.

content_text:
## Things to know
Complete this section if you're an Australian resident for tax purposes who either:
- had either a direct or an indirect interest in a controlled foreign company (CFC)
- at any time, directly or indirectly transferred or caused the transfer of property (including money) or services to a non-resident trust.
If you're a temporary resident, don't show your foreign investment income at this section. See Tax-free income for temporary residents for the definition of a temporary resident and details of the exemption.
### Controlled foreign company (CFC) income
The CFC measures may apply to income and gains of foreign companies in which you alone, or with associates, had either:
- direct or indirect controlling interest totalling 10% or more in a foreign company
- effective control of a foreign company.
For more information, see Attribution of the current year profits of a controlled foreign company (CFC) .
### Income from transfer of property (including money) or services to a non-resident trust estate
The transferor trust measures may apply if, at any time, you directly or indirectly transferred or caused the transfer of property (including money) or services to a non-resident trust. A trust is a non-resident trust if at any time during the income year both of the following applied:
- no trustee of the trust estate was an Australian resident
- the trust estate's central management and control wasn't in Australia.
For more information, see Transferor trust and related measures .
## Completing this section
To personalise your tax return to show foreign entities, at Personalise return select:
- You had foreign income
- Foreign entities
To show your foreign entities details, at Prepare return select 'Add/Edit' at the Foreign income, assets and entities banner.
At the Foreign entities banner:
- Enter your attributed foreign income from any CFC at Controlled foreign company (CFC) income .
- Enter your attributed foreign income from transferor trusts at Income from transfer of property (including money) or services to a non-resident trust estate .
- If you enter an amount at Income from transfer of property (including money) or services to a non-resident trust estate , you need to enter the Name of non-resident trust Name of trustee or trustees .
- Select Save .
- Select Save and continue when you have completed the Foreign income, assets and entities section.
source_title: myTax 2026 Foreign entities | Australian Taxation Office
source_description: How to complete your tax return using myTax when you have a direct or indirect interest in a foreign entity. Published 1 June 2026.
